name: claude-for-codex
description: Use Claude Code from Codex for read-only review, adversarial review, and planning.

interface.shortDescription: Claude Code reviews, planning, and stop-gate workflows for Codex.
interface.longDescription: Claude for Codex connects Codex to the local Claude Code CLI for independent read-only code review, adversarial design critique, implementation planning, multi-role review fan-out, and an opt-in Stop hook review gate. It is designed for multi-model collaboration: Codex implements and reconciles, while Claude provides a separate reviewer or planner perspective. Claude invocations are constrained to read-only tools for review workflows, and the Stop gate fails open on Claude runtime errors unless Claude explicitly returns a blocking verdict.
defaultPrompt: Use Claude to review the current git changes.
Ask Claude to challenge this implementation plan.
Run a multi-role Claude review across correctness, security, tests, release, and adversarial perspectives.
Enable the opt-in Claude Stop review gate for this repository.
Run a Codex-Claude collaboration loop and reconcile the findings.
